scenes.title: Python Concurrency Visualizer
scenes.subtitle: Comparing Concurrency and Parallelism paradigms in Python.
scenes.label: Scene 1 / 6
stepSubtitles: Welcome to the Concurrency Paradigm Visualizer.
We will compare Sync, Threads, Async, and Multiprocess models.
Press play or next to begin.

scenes.title: Synchronous Execution
scenes.subtitle: Single-thread running tasks sequentially. Blocking blocks everything.
scenes.label: Scene 2 / 6
stepSubtitles: Synchronous Single-Thread: Initiating Task 1...
Task 1 executes step 1.
Task 1 executes step 2.
Task 1 completes. Initiating Task 2...
Task 2 executes step 1.
Task 2 executes step 2.
Both tasks complete sequentially. Total duration: Sum of Task 1 + Task 2.

scenes.title: Multithreading (Preemptive)
scenes.subtitle: Threads share memory but run concurrently via time-slicing due to the GIL.
scenes.label: Scene 3 / 6
stepSubtitles: Multithreading: Initiating Thread A and Thread B concurrently.
Thread A acquires the GIL and runs first step...
Thread A yields (e.g. waits on I/O). Thread B acquires GIL and runs step 1...
Thread B yields. Thread A re-acquires GIL and runs step 2...
Thread A completes and releases GIL. Thread B runs step 2...
Thread B completes. Concurrency is achieved through preemptive time-slicing.

scenes.title: Asynchronous (Cooperative)
scenes.subtitle: A single thread uses an Event Loop to schedule cooperative tasks (coroutine await).
scenes.label: Scene 4 / 6
stepSubtitles: Asynchronous: Event Loop initiates Coroutine 1 and Coroutine 2.
Coroutine 1 executes until it encounters an 'await' blocking statement...
Coroutine 1 yields control. The Event Loop switches to Coroutine 2...
Coroutine 2 executes until it encounters an 'await' statement and yields...
The Event Loop checks and resumes Coroutine 1 to execute its final step.
The Event Loop resumes Coroutine 2 to complete its final step. All on one thread!

scenes.title: Multiprocessing (Parallel)
scenes.subtitle: Spawns separate OS processes with individual GILs, running in parallel.
scenes.label: Scene 5 / 6
stepSubtitles: Multiprocessing: Spawning Process A (Core 1) and Process B (Core 2) in parallel.
Process A and Process B begin executing on separate cores simultaneously.
Process A runs step 1 on Core 1 | Process B runs step 1 on Core 2 in parallel.
Process A runs step 2 on Core 1 | Process B runs step 2 on Core 2 in parallel.
Process A completes | Process B completes.
True CPU parallelism is achieved, completely bypassing CPython's single GIL bottleneck.

scenes.title: Summary Matrix
scenes.subtitle: Choosing the correct model based on performance bottlenecks.
scenes.label: Scene 6 / 6
stepSubtitles: Sync: Best for simple, short scripts with no blocking operations.
Threading: Best for multiple, blocking network/file I/O calls.
Asyncio: Best for high-concurrency web servers/sockets on a single thread.
Multiprocessing: Best for heavy CPU calculations, machine learning, and image operations.
Choose the paradigm that matches your performance bottlenecks.
